page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance family server featuring 12 shared vCPUs mapped to 12 physical Intel Xeon Cascadelake cores running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results place this server in the average performance tier across most standard workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU score is 1418.58, while the multi-core score reaches 17309.27. Memory bandwidth tests show 1224.9 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 87.74 GB/sec for large-block reads and 47.62 GB/sec for large-block writes. The system demonstrates solid database capabilities with a PassMark database operations score of 4095.21 and Redis SET performance of 18.53 million operations per second. However, LLM inference on a medium 7B model is limited, yielding a weak 7.43 tokens per second for text generation.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff, as resource contention may affect sustained high-compute tasks compared to dedicated instances. However, this shared model offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that do not require constant 100% CPU utilization. This server is well-suited for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose applications that benefit from a high core count and fast NVMe storage without the premium cost of dedicated hardware.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 12 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It features 24.0 GB of RAM, a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 17309.27 and a PassMark database score of 4095.21. The instance delivers strong Redis SET throughput at 18.53 million operations per second and static web serving at 11.99 GB/sec. However, LLM inference on 7B models is weak, reflecting the constraints of the shared CPU model. This server offers a cost-efficient balance of high core count and fast local storage, making it suitable for multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and moderate database workloads where dedicated CPU performance is not strictly required.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance server with 12 shared Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced multi-threaded performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel features 12 shared Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, optimized for cost-effective multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A cost-effective, multi-core virtual server featuring fast NVMe storage and balanced memory for web serving and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
High-throughput static web serving at 11.99 GB/sec
Efficient Redis performance with 18.53 million SET operations per second
Best suited for multi-threaded decompression, web hosting, and moderate databases
